rules_text: Reveal one Blue Team Asset. If it is Infrastructure or Application, draw 1 card.
flavor_text: The open web remembers what the utility forgot.
learning_note: Attackers often begin by looking for exposed services and public-facing systems.

rules_text: Reveal up to 2 Blue Team Assets. If Basic Firewall is not active, draw 1 card.
flavor_text: The noise starts before the breach.
learning_note: Basic scanning can identify services, ports, and exposed systems before an attack.

rules_text: Deal 2 damage. If the target has Telnet, HTTP, Port 23, or Port 80 attached, deal +1 damage.
flavor_text: admin/admin still opens doors.
learning_note: Default credentials remain a common risk for poorly managed or legacy systems.

rules_text: Deal 2 damage. If Basic MFA is not active, disable one Operator until next turn.
flavor_text: One password tried everywhere is still one too many.
learning_note: Password spraying targets weak authentication without needing advanced exploitation.

rules_text: If Basic MFA is not active, bypass one Defense card played against this Attack.
flavor_text: Convenience becomes a doorway when nobody locks it.
learning_note: Remote access should be protected with MFA, logging, and access restrictions.

rules_text: Deal 3 damage to one Legacy, Unsupported, or Endpoint Asset.
flavor_text: Copied code can still cause real damage.
learning_note: Known vulnerabilities can be dangerous when affected systems remain exposed or unpatched.

rules_text: Disable one Operator for 1 turn unless Blue Team discards 1 card.
flavor_text: The inbox became the entry point.
learning_note: Social engineering can disrupt response by targeting operational staff.

rules_text: Deal 3 damage to an Application Asset. If SCADA Web Interface is in play, draw 1 card.
flavor_text: A useful dashboard can become a useful target.
learning_note: Exposed administrative and monitoring interfaces increase risk when access control is weak.

rules_text: Deal 3 damage. If Port Scan or Internet Exposure Search was played this turn, deal +1 damage.
flavor_text: Skill optional. Exposure required.
learning_note: Low-skill attackers can still cause harm when public exploit code matches exposed systems.

rules_text: Deal 4 damage to an Endpoint Asset. Blue Team may draw 1 card after this Attack resolves.
flavor_text: Loud attacks can still hurt.
learning_note: Commodity malware may be detectable but can still create disruption if response is slow.

rules_text: After damaging one Asset, deal 1 damage to another Asset unless Network Segmentation is active.
flavor_text: One open path became two.
learning_note: Flat networks can allow attackers to move between systems more easily.

rules_text: If Default Login Attempt or Password Spray was played this game, deal +2 damage.
flavor_text: Old passwords have long memories.
learning_note: Credential reuse increases the blast radius of one compromised account.

rules_text: Deal 3 damage to one Asset. Patch Window or Patch Management reduces this damage by 2.
flavor_text: The fix existed before the outage did.
learning_note: Patch delays can leave systems exposed to known weaknesses.

rules_text: Deal 2 damage. If Port Closure is not active, reveal Blue Team's hand.
flavor_text: The door was not hidden. It was open.
learning_note: Unnecessary exposed services can help attackers understand and target an environment.

rules_text: Credential Attacks deal +1 damage until the end of this turn.
flavor_text: A short password can make a long incident.
learning_note: Weak password policies increase risk from guessing, reuse, and credential attacks.

rules_text: Blue Team must choose: discard 1 Defense card or Red Team draws 1 card.
flavor_text: Too much noise hides the signal.
learning_note: High alert volume can slow response and cause important events to be missed.

rules_text: Deal 2 damage to an Application Asset. If Asset Inventory is not active, draw 1 card.
flavor_text: Temporary pages have a way of becoming permanent.
learning_note: Forgotten web pages and test interfaces can expose useful information.

rules_text: Deal 2 damage. If Backup or Cloud Backup is active, reduce one Recovery card's effect by 1 this turn.
flavor_text: Backups help recovery unless they become part of the attack.
learning_note: Backup storage should be protected and access-controlled to preserve recovery options.

rules_text: Deal 3 damage. Vendor Support Contract reduces this damage by 1; Vendor Access Review reduces it by 2.
flavor_text: The contractor left. The account stayed.
learning_note: Unused third-party accounts create risk when access is not reviewed or removed.

rules_text: Deal 1 damage. Blue Team loses 1 SCP unless Incident Response Playbook or IT Admin is active.
flavor_text: The damage was small. The phone calls were not.
learning_note: Public-facing incidents can create communication and trust impacts beyond technical damage.

rules_text: Red Team draws 2 cards. Blue Team may draw 1 Defense card.
flavor_text: The attack was quiet until the attacker wanted credit.
learning_note: Public claims and noisy attacker behavior can create defender intelligence opportunities.

rules_text: Deal 2 damage to Operator Laptop or Engineering Workstation.
flavor_text: The workstation was old in more ways than one.
learning_note: Outdated endpoint software can expose operational users to preventable compromise.

rules_text: Deal 2 damage. If Basic Firewall is active, exhaust it until Blue Team's next turn.
flavor_text: A wall with the wrong hole is still a hole.
learning_note: Firewall rules require review because overly broad access can defeat good architecture.

rules_text: Reveal one Infrastructure Asset and one Protocol attached to it.
flavor_text: Status pages love to overshare.
learning_note: Public status or diagnostic pages can leak operational details useful for targeting.

rules_text: Play one additional Attack this turn. That Attack deals -1 damage.
flavor_text: Automation makes small skill move faster.
learning_note: Low-skill attackers often rely on automation to increase speed and volume.

rules_text: Deal 3 damage to an Asset with Telnet, HTTP, Modbus TCP, Port 23, or Port 80 attached.
flavor_text: Legacy paths invite modern trouble.
learning_note: Older protocols can require compensating controls when they lack modern security features.

rules_text: Return one Common Attack from your discard pile to your hand.
flavor_text: The second try is cheaper than the first.
learning_note: Repeated low-skill attacks can still create pressure when defenses are not improved.

rules_text: Provides 1 Internet resource.
flavor_text: The attack starts from what is already public.
learning_note: Low-skill attackers often begin from public exposure and open services.

rules_text: Provides 1 Internet resource OR 1 Application resource.
flavor_text: A cheap platform gives the attacker a place to start.
learning_note: Shared hosting, exposed apps, and public web paths often shape opportunistic attacks.

rules_text: Provides 1 Internet resource OR 1 Network resource.
flavor_text: The remote path was supposed to help support, not attackers.
learning_note: Remote access exposure is a common bridge from internet discovery to internal impact.

rules_text: Provides 1 Endpoint resource OR 1 Application resource.
flavor_text: One weak workstation becomes a launchpad.
learning_note: Endpoint compromise can support both malware and application-layer attacks.

rules_text: Provides 1 Network resource OR 1 Infrastructure resource.
flavor_text: The account lived longer than the contract.
learning_note: Vendor access can bridge network access to operational systems when not reviewed.

rules_text: Provides 1 Endpoint resource OR 1 Internet resource.
flavor_text: The tool was downloaded before it was understood.
learning_note: Commodity tools let low-skill attackers move quickly when targets are exposed.

rules_text: Provides 1 Application resource OR 1 Network resource.
flavor_text: The password was not stolen here, but it still worked here.
learning_note: Credential reuse and leaked credentials often enable basic but impactful attacks.

rules_text: Provides 1 Legacy resource OR 1 Application resource.
flavor_text: Old data creates new trouble.
learning_note: Leaked information can help attackers target legacy services and reused accounts.
